Stephen L. Collier; Tenderloin Housing Clinic, Inc.; 415-771-9850; appearing as counsel for plaintiff Rodrigo Mendoza Luna.
Brett Gibbs; trn Law Associates; 415-823-4566; appearing as counsel for defendants Behrouz Halali and Behnam Halali.
Tiffany Norman; trn Law Associates; 415-823-4566; appearing as counsel for defendants Behrouz Halali and Behnam Halali.

Defendants contest and seek clarification as to the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION is GRANTED. Defendants and all persons acting on their behalf are restrained from doing, directly or indirectly the following acts pending the resolution of this action or further order of this Court:
(1) blocking Plaintiff's (and family) access to any common areas unless access is interrupted temporarily for a lawful purpose (e.g. for repairs), for a reasonable amount of time, and with a reasonable notice;
(2) changing the locks to Plaintiff's unit or common areas unless the key is immediately provided to the Plaintiff if the locks are changed when Plaintiff is at the premises or Defendants make the keys available to the Plaintiff for pick up immediately upon Plaintiff's return to the premises (e.g. Plaintiff's return from work, vacation, etc.);
(3) terminating Plaintiff's housing services, including, but not limited to utilities, cable, internet, etc.
